evals.prompt: hey do i have any notes on docker or containers? been meaning to check what i've saved
evals.expected_output: A clean list of notes found (title, path, excerpt) with an offer to read any in full. Should use mcp__engram__search_notes.
expectations: Called mcp__engram__search_notes with a relevant query
Results are displayed as a clean list with title, path, and a short excerpt
Offered to read any note in full
Did not dump raw note content without being asked

evals.prompt: search my vault for anything about health, supplements, or fitness
evals.expected_output: Should detect 'health' and 'supplements' as category words, call list_tags to find relevant tags, then filter search results by tags. Presents clean results.
expectations: Called mcp__engram__list_tags because the query contains obvious category words (health, supplements)
Re-ran search with relevant tag filters based on available tags
Results are displayed with title, path, and excerpt
Offered to read any result in full

evals.prompt: I'm thinking about picking up the phoenix protocol again, any thoughts on where to start?
evals.expected_output: Claude should silently search 'phoenix protocol' before answering, absorb whatever it finds, and respond informed by vault context — without announcing 'let me search your vault' or asking what it is.
expectations: Called mcp__engram__search_notes proactively without being asked to
Did NOT announce the search or ask the user what 'phoenix protocol' is before searching
Response is informed by whatever notes were found (or gracefully pivoted to a clarifying question if nothing was found)
Search was conducted silently — no phrases like 'let me check your vault' or 'I'll search your notes'
